preamble: The diphthongization of [ã] to [oi] is a well-documented process in Romance languages, particularly Portuguese, where historical nasal vowels underwent significant restructuring.

examples.note: Historical development of nasalized vowels in various positions often resulted in [oi] diphthongs.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
